events.text: Patient is a 61-year-old Asian American male, height 5'6", weight 155 lbs, BMI 25.0, never smoker, no significant past medical history, family history of gastric cancer in mother.
At age 53, patient was diagnosed with prostate adenocarcinoma (ICD-O site C61.9, histology 8140/3) after an elevated PSA of 6.2 ng/mL found on routine physical examination.
At age 53, MRI-targeted prostate biopsy showed Gleason 3+4=7 (Grade Group 2) adenocarcinoma in 4 of 12 cores from the left peripheral zone; PI-RADS 4 lesion; no perineural invasion.
At age 53, staging with CT and bone scan was negative for metastatic disease; staged as cT2aN0M0 favorable intermediate-risk prostate cancer.

At age 53, patient underwent robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection; pathologic stage pT2cN0M0 with negative surgical margins and 0 of 14 lymph nodes positive.
At age 53, prostatectomy specimen confirmed Gleason 3+4=7 adenocarcinoma confined to the prostate bilaterally with negative margins and no extraprostatic extension.
At age 53, postoperative PSA was undetectable at less than 0.01 ng/mL; patient was placed on surveillance.
At age 56, PSA rose to 0.6 ng/mL confirming biochemical recurrence; restaging imaging was negative.
At age 56, salvage radiation therapy was delivered to the prostate bed, total dose 66 Gy in 33 fractions.
At age 57, post-salvage radiation PSA nadir was 0.08 ng/mL.
At age 59, PSA rose to 2.8 ng/mL representing second biochemical recurrence after salvage radiation.
At age 59, leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was initiated for second biochemical recurrence.
At age 59, PSMA PET/CT performed for staging showed PSMA-avid lesions in L2 and L4 vertebral bodies, right sacroiliac joint, and left 7th rib; no visceral disease; SUVmax ranged from 6.4 to 11.8.
At age 59, disease reclassified as metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer following identification of bone metastases on PSMA PET with PSA of 2.8 ng/mL and testosterone of 28 ng/dL on ADT.
At age 59, enzalutamide 160 mg orally daily was added to leuprolide for mCRPC.
At age 59, after 3 months on enzalutamide, PSA declined to 0.9 ng/mL.

At age 59, next-generation sequencing (FoundationOne CDx) on archival prostatectomy tissue revealed CDK12 biallelic loss (frameshift and missense), SPOP F133L mutation, and FOXA1 R219Q mutation; BRCA1/2 wild type; ATM wild type; no homologous recombination deficiency; tumor mutational burden 7 mutations/Mb; microsatellite stable.
At age 60, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 cognitive impairment (difficulty with word-finding) attributed to enzalutamide; discussed with patient who elected to continue therapy.
At age 60, restaging PSMA PET/CT showed stable bone disease with no new lesions; PSA was 0.7 ng/mL.
At age 61, PSA rose to 4.2 ng/mL on enzalutamide; imaging showed new PSMA-avid lesion in the right 4th rib; enzalutamide was discontinued.
At age 61, docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated following progression on enzalutamide.
At age 61, after cycle 3 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia (ANC 1100/uL), grade 2 mucositis, and grade 1 alopecia; treatment continued with supportive care.
At age 61, after 4 cycles of docetaxel, restaging CT and bone scan showed mixed response with improvement in some lesions but a new focus in the left ilium; PSA was 3.8 ng/mL.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 53, MRI-targeted prostate biopsy showed Gleason 3+4=7 (Grade Group 2) adenocarcinoma in 4 of 12 cores from the left peripheral zone; PI-RADS 4 lesion; no perineural invasion.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 53, staging with CT and bone scan was negative for metastatic disease; staged as cT2aN0M0 favorable intermediate-risk prostate cancer.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). Rising PSA on ADT. Treated with enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, or cabazitaxel. Include BRCA1/2 and homologous recombination deficiency testing. Some with bone-predominant and some with visceral metastases.
